title: Benzinga Pro Pricing Explained: All Plans, Costs & Fees (2026)

content:
I'll write a comprehensive pricing deep-dive using the tool data you provided. Let me structure this as HTML content.

Benzinga Pro Pricing Overview

Benzinga Pro is a real-time news and alerts platform designed for active traders who prioritize breaking market information over technical analysis. Starting at $37 per month, Benzinga Pro offers three pricing tiers that scale with feature access, though the pricing structure creates significant jumps between entry and mid-tier options.

Founded in 2010 and based in Detroit, Michigan, Benzinga has built its reputation on delivering market-moving headlines faster than competing platforms. The platform focuses exclusively on real-time news delivery, alerts, and stock screening—not charting or trade execution. This specialized positioning directly impacts its pricing strategy, which aims at news-driven traders rather than all-in-one platform seekers.

All Benzinga Pro Plans Compared

Benzinga Pro offers three subscription tiers with increasing feature access. Here's the complete breakdown:

Plan Tier Monthly Price Key Inclusions
Basic $37/month Real-time news feed, basic stock screening, mobile alerts, educational content, watchlist functionality
Streamlined $147/month All Basic features, advanced news filters and keyword search, priority mobile notifications, extended market coverage (stocks, options, crypto), enhanced screening capabilities
Essential $197/month All Streamlined features, audio squawk streams, live market commentary, multi-asset alerts (stocks, options, crypto), premium educational resources

Important Note: The pricing data provided reflects monthly billing rates. Annual pricing or discounts are not specified in the available information, so we cannot confirm whether Benzinga Pro offers cost savings for annual prepayment—a standard industry practice among trading platforms.

Free Plan / Free Trial

Benzinga Pro does not offer a free tier. Unlike many competitors (TD Ameritrade's thinkorswim, for example, which offers free access with a brokerage account), Benzinga requires a paid subscription to access any platform features.

Trial availability is not documented in the current product information. If you're considering Benzinga Pro, you may want to contact their sales team directly to inquire about a trial period—many news and alert platforms offer 7-14 day trials to let traders evaluate whether the platform fits their workflow.

Bottom line: Expect to commit to a paid subscription immediately. The $37/month Basic tier is the lowest entry point, making it the natural starting plan for evaluating whether Benzinga Pro's speed and news focus justify the cost for your trading strategy.

Hidden Costs and Fees

Benzinga Pro's pricing is notably transparent—what you see is what you pay monthly. However, traders should understand what's not included:

  • No exchange data feeds included: Real-time stock, options, and crypto data are bundled into the subscription (no separate Level 2 quotes or market data fees are mentioned), but this should be confirmed directly with Benzinga.
  • No charting or technical analysis: If you need candlestick charts, moving averages, or pattern recognition, you'll need a separate platform—often adding $50-$200/month to your toolkit.
  • No trade execution: Benzinga is a news and alerts tool only. You must execute trades through a separate broker (TD Ameritrade, Webull, Interactive Brokers, etc.), potentially with its own fees or commissions depending on your account type.
  • No API or backtesting: The Essential tier does not provide API access or historical backtesting capabilities, limiting automation for algorithmic traders.
  • Add-on pricing unclear: Whether premium data feeds, extended hours alerts, or other add-ons incur additional fees is not specified in publicly available information.

For active traders, the true cost of using Benzinga Pro should include the price of a charting platform (like TradingView at $15/month minimum) and potentially a broker commission structure—making total platform costs range from $50-$300+/month depending on your setup.

Benzinga Pro Pricing vs Competitors

To evaluate whether Benzinga Pro's pricing is competitive, compare it to similar news-focused and screening platforms:

  • Finviz Elite: Approximately $40/month for stock screening and basic news. Slightly cheaper than Benzinga Basic, but Finviz lacks audio squawk and real-time alert speed—Benzinga's primary differentiators.
  • Trade Ideas: Approximately $99-$299/month depending on tier. Competes directly with Benzinga on screening and alerts, but Trade Ideas includes AI-powered stock scanner and backtesting—features Benzinga lacks. Benzinga compensates with faster news delivery and audio squawk functionality.
  • TD Ameritrade thinkorswim: Free with a brokerage account (or $100/month standalone). Offers charting, screening, and execution all-in-one, but lacks Benzinga's news speed advantage and doesn't specialize in breaking alerts the way Benzinga does.

Pricing positioning: Benzinga Pro sits in the middle ground—more expensive than basic screeners like Finviz, but cheaper than all-in-one platforms. The pricing justification hinges entirely on whether you value speed and news exclusivity enough to pay for a dedicated platform.

Is Benzinga Pro Worth the Price?

Benzinga Pro's value proposition varies dramatically by trader type:

Basic Tier ($37/month) — Best For: News-driven swing traders and day traders who trade catalysts. If your strategy relies on reacting to FDA approvals, earnings surprises, or SEC filings before the crowd, the $37 entry price is justified. The real-time news feed and mobile alerts are purpose-built for this workflow. This tier makes sense if you're willing to use a separate platform for charting.

Streamlined Tier ($147/month) — Best For: Active traders managing multiple watchlists across stocks, options, and crypto simultaneously. Advanced filters and keyword search become valuable when you're monitoring 20+ symbols and need to cut through noise. The $110 monthly increase over Basic is steep, however—evaluate whether the added filtering is worth the cost versus using Basic + a secondary screening tool.

Essential Tier ($197/month) — Difficult to justify for most traders. The audio squawk and live market commentary add value for multi-monitor day traders, but $197/month is positioning Benzinga Pro in premium territory without the technical analysis or execution capabilities of competing all-in-one platforms. Unless audio squawk is mission-critical to your trading, Streamlined is likely sufficient.

Value verdict: Benzinga Pro delivers genuine differentiation in real-time news speed and exclusive breaking stories. However, the pricing jumps (Basic to Streamlined is a 297% increase) and absence of charting/execution mean most traders will use Benzinga Pro as one component of a multi-tool setup, not a standalone platform. Total platform costs typically exceed $100/month when paired with charting.

How to Save on Benzinga Pro

Annual billing discounts: Not specified in available information. Check directly with Benzinga's sales team—many platforms offer 10-20% savings for annual prepayment, which could reduce Basic tier to approximately $31-33/month if available.

Student or educator discounts: Not documented. Benzinga's trader focus and price point suggest student discounts are unlikely, but it's worth asking if you qualify.

Promotional codes: Benzinga periodically runs promotions, typically advertised during earnings season (January, April, July, October). Subscribe to their newsletter or check trading forums for current offers.

Cost optimization strategy: Instead of immediately upgrading from Basic to Streamlined ($110/month increase), test whether the Basic tier satisfies your alerts and screening needs for 2-3 months. Many traders find that adding a complementary tool (like Finviz for advanced screening at $40/month) costs less than upgrading to Streamlined while maintaining the specialized news focus Benzinga excels at.

Broker integration: Confirm whether your broker offers Benzinga Pro bundled or discounted. Some prop trading firms include news subscriptions in their platform fees—worth checking before paying full retail.

Final Takeaway

Benzinga Pro's $37-$197/month pricing reflects the market value of real-time financial news and alerts in retail trading. The Basic tier is genuinely affordable for news-focused traders; the Essential tier is difficult to justify without significant volume or specialized trading approach. Focus on starting with Basic, validating that Benzinga's news speed meaningfully improves your trading, and only upgrading if specific Advanced features directly increase your edge—not simply because they're available.
